text: Badlands National Park
zh: 巴德兰兹国家公园

text: This great, good land. This is what America is.
zh: 这片辽阔而美好的土地，这就是美国。

text: But America is also its badlands.
zh: 但美国也有它的恶地。

text: Moonscape regions fantastically shaped and reshaped by the relentless forces of erosion,
zh: 月球般的地貌，经过侵蚀力量无休止的雕琢与重塑，形态奇特。

text: blasting winds, pounding rains.
zh: 狂风肆虐，暴雨如注。

text: Why are these lands called bad? You need only step off an established trail to find out.
zh: 为什么这些土地被称为“恶地”？你只需踏出既定的小径便能明白。

text: A terrain torturous to travel over. Badlands. Bad to cross.
zh: 这是一片让人举步维艰的地形。恶地，难以穿越。

text: The most famous and infamous of America's badlands are those of the Dakotas. home of the Black Hills,
zh: 美国最著名也最恶名昭彰的恶地位于达科他州，那里是黑山的所在地，

text: Mount Rushmore National Monument, and two flourishing national parks.
zh: 拉什莫尔山国家纪念地，以及两座蓬勃发展的国家公园。

text: Buttes, pinnacles, spires with layers of subtly colored rock,
zh: 平顶山、尖峰和塔状岩石层层叠叠，色彩微妙，

text: beige, pink, green, and gold.
zh: 米色、粉色、绿色和金色交织其中。

text: This is Badlands National Park in southwest South Dakota.
zh: 这就是位于南达科他州西南部的巴德兰兹国家公园。

text: Every year, over three quarter million people visit the park's 240 ,000 acres of buttes, pinnacles and spires.
zh: 每年有超过七十五万人前来参观这片占地二十四万英亩的平顶山、尖峰与岩塔。

text: Badlands is also its vast prairies.
zh: 恶地同样拥有广袤的草原。

text: This is a remnant of one of the world's great grasslands.
zh: 这是世界上最伟大的草原之一的遗迹。

text: Some 2 ,000 years ago, these grasslands were dense with bison.
zh: 大约两千年前，这片草原上曾遍布野牛。

text: The American buffalo. An estimated 60 million of them roamed the North American plains.
zh: 美洲野牛。据估计，曾有六千万头野牛漫游在北美大平原上。

text: For thousands of years, Native Americans hunted them.
zh: 数千年来，美洲原住民一直在猎捕它们。

text: The bison provided everything they needed.
zh: 野牛为他们提供了生活所需的一切。

text: Hides for robes, clothes, drum heads, teepee coverings,
zh: 兽皮制成长袍、衣物、鼓面和帐篷覆盖物，

text: sinew for bow strings, horn for cooking utensils.
zh: 筋腱用作弓弦，牛角制成炊具。

text: Clearly, the bison sustained their very existence.
zh: 显然，野牛支撑着他们的生存。

text: the bison were almost annihilated by white hunters.
zh: 然而，野牛几乎被白人猎人赶尽杀绝。

text: Wiping Sipping out the buffalo as a food source was a way of starving the Indians into submission.
zh: 消灭作为食物来源的野牛，是迫使印第安人屈服的一种手段。

text: President and conservationist Theodore Roosevelt once commented sadly,
zh: 总统兼自然保护主义者西奥多·罗斯福曾感慨地说道：

text: "...never before were so many large, wild animals of one species slain in so short a space of time."
zh: “……从未有过如此多的大型野生动物，在如此短的时间内被大规模屠杀。”

text: Once powerful tribes, still proud, were forced to live on handouts.
zh: 曾经强大而依然自豪的部落，如今却被迫依靠施舍度日。

text: Families of starving Sioux Indians would perform the Ghost Dance.
zh: 饥饿的苏族印第安家庭会跳起“幽灵舞”。

text: In their despair, many of them turned to this ancient ritual that they believed would magically bring back the buffalo.
zh: 在绝望中，许多人求助于这一古老仪式，相信它能神奇地唤回野牛。

text: Today, the buffalo have returned to the Badlands,
zh: 如今，野牛已重返巴德兰兹，

text: due more to movements begun by people like Theodore Roosevelt than to silenced songs of the Ghost Dancers.
zh: 这更多归功于西奥多·罗斯福等人发起的保护运动，而非那已沉寂的幽灵舞之歌。

text: And today, these lands are more hospitable to outsiders than they were when Native Americans roamed here.
zh: 如今，这片土地对外来者而言，比原住民曾经生活于此时更加友善。

text: Weaving through narrow passes, the Badlands Loop Road offers roadside exhibits and several scenic overlooks.
zh: 恶地环形公路蜿蜒穿过狭窄的峡道，沿途设有展示牌和几处绝佳的观景点。

text: This is the only paved road in the park. There's wildlife at every turn.
zh: 这是公园内唯一的铺装道路。转过每个弯都能看到野生动物。

text: Prairie dogs, creatures unknown until one was captured
zh: 草原犬鼠，这种生物直到被人捕获才为世人所知，

text: by Lewis and Clark, and sent to President Thomas Jefferson along with a progress report.
zh: 那是刘易斯和克拉克所捕获，并连同一份考察报告一起送往托马斯·杰斐逊总统。

text: Wild turkeys. Big Horn Sheep.
zh: 野火鸡、大角羊。

text: The park's White River Badlands are renowned, renowned internationally
zh: 公园内的白河恶地地区在国际上享有盛誉，

text: as one of the most important fossil -rich areas on our planet.
zh: 被誉为地球上最重要的富含化石的地区之一。

text: Some 67 to 75 million years ago, this prairie was the soggy bottom of an ancient sea.
zh: 大约六千七百万到七千五百万年前，这片草原曾是古代海洋泥泞的海底。

text: From this black, muddy floor, scientists have uncovered fossils of bizarre sea creatures.
zh: 科学家们从这片黑色泥泞的地层中，发掘出许多奇异海洋生物的化石。

text: Some millions of years later, volcanic eruptions buried the area with successive layers of ash.
zh: 数百万年后，火山喷发接连不断，将这片区域层层掩埋在火山灰之下。

text: A grave for prehistoric beasts, but for today's scientists, a treasure trove of fossils.
zh: 这里是史前巨兽的坟场，却是当今科学家眼中的化石宝库。

text: To park visitors, as well as to probing scientists, these badlands proved to be uniquely good.
zh: 无论对公园游客，还是对孜孜探索的科学家而言，这片恶地都被证明是独一无二的美好之地。
